Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. ROSEMARY S ALLRED
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 6, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s unopposed motion for summary judgment is granted. A party may move for summary adjudication in an action or proceeding if it is contended that the cause of action has no merit. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 437c(a)(1).) The motion for summary adjudication shall be granted if all the papers submitted show that there is no triable issue as to any material fact. (Id., sec. 437c(c).) The moving party must make an initial showing, based upon affirmative evidence, that summary adjudication must be granted. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Company (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) If the movant meets this initial burden, the burden then shifts to the opposing party to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists. The court liberally construes the evidence in support of the party opposing summary adjudication and resolves doubts concerning the evidence in favor of that party. (Hughes v. Pair (2009) 46 Cal.4th 1035, 1039.) The moving party bears the burden of persuasion "from the commencement to conclusion" to show "that there is no triable issue of material fact and that [it] is entitled to [summary adjudication] as a matter of law." (Ibid.) Here, Wells Fargo has produced evidence sufficient to show it is entitled to judgment on its breach of contract cause of action and its common counts / money lent cause of action. The court notes that several hundred pages of exhibit 2 are blank. This exhibit includes statements showing use of the credit card at issue by defendant Rosemary Allred. It is not clear whether there are statements missing or whether the blank pages were an administrative error. But the court concludes that even without these exhibits the Reed declaration demonstrates that Wells Fargo has presented evidence from which a trier of fact could find it has met its burden on each cause of action, and Allred presents no evidence to rebut or undermine its showing. The court orders judgment be entered in favor of Wells Fargo. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JOSH ANDERSON VS. AMY BEASOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Answers Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 11, 1 - PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON'S Motion For Order Compelling Answers Form Interrogatories Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his form interrogatories, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part. Anderson obtained an order of this court on January 17, 2025, compelling defendant Amy Beasom to respond, without objections, to the form interrogatories (along with special interrogatories, set one, and requests for production of documents, set one) by February 18, 2025. Beasom served verified responses dated February 17, 2025. (Sanders Dec. Ex. 4.) Anderson's meet and confer letter of February 28 pointed out deficiencies in the responses and requested supplementation by March 14, 2025. (Sanders Dec. Ex. 5.) On March 14, 2025, Beasom sent updated responses through counsel to Anderson's counsel's email address. (Young Dec. para. 3 & unnumbered exhibit.) Anderson apparently did not receive them and contends they are inadequate in any event. The court orders Beasom to provide further responses to the following form interrogatories: 2.7; 15.1(c). The court otherwise finds supplemental Beasom's responses in substantial compliance with her discovery obligation. Beasom shall amend, with verifications, by May 15, 2025. Anderson seeks sanctions. The court finds Beasom's opposition to the motion to be substantially justified and does not award monetary or evidentiary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JOSH ANDERSON VS. AMY BEASOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Answers Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 11, 2 - PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON'S Motion For Order Compelling Answers Special Interrogatories Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his special interrogatories, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part. The relevant factual history and procedural history are set out in the court's companion order concerning the motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories. The court orders Beasom to provide further responses to the following special interrogatories: 20, 21, 23, 27, and 29. The court otherwise finds Beasom's supplemental responses in substantial compliance with her discovery obligations. Anderson largely objects to Beasom's other special interrogatory responses (including her supplemental responses) because he does not credit her representation that she no longer has documents or seeks further detail in her answers. A party's response that it lacks information is full and complete, even if the opposing party does not find the answer credible, and does not provide a basis to order further responses. Anderson seeks sanctions. The court orders a reduced amount in light of Anderson's partial success; Beasom shall pay $250 to Anderson by May 15, 2025. Beasom's position here was largely substantially justified but Beasom has not yet responded to some of the interrogatories identified in Anderson's moving papers and on that basis is sanctioned. (See CCP 2030.290(c).) The court denies Anderson's request for evidentiary sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: JOSH ANDERSON VS. AMY BEASOM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Compelling Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 11, 3 - PLAINTIFF JOSH ANDERSON Motion For Order Compelling Production Of Documents (tentative ruling part 1 of 2) Plaintiff Josh Anderson moves to compel further responses to his requests for production of documents, set one, and for monetary and evidentiary sanctions. For the reasons stated herein, the court grants the motion in part and denies it in part. The relevant factual history and procedural history are set out in the court's companion order concerning the motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories. Beasom has provided objection-free verified supplemental responses. Her responses aver that she has only two documents related to her mortgage in her possession, custody, or control. Beasom's obligation to produce documents extends to documents she can readily obtain from her agents such as a mortgage broker. It is not clear whether Beasom has made a diligent inquiry. In addition, CCP 2031.230 provides that "A representation of inability to comply with the particular demand for inspection ... shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand. This statement shall also specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer, in the possession, custody, or control of the responding party. The statement shall set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control of that item or category of item." Here, where Anderson's RFPs relate to categories of documents that likely exist somewhere, such as documents about Beasom's bills, mortgage, and refinancing, even if they are no longer in Beasom's possession, Beasom is obliged to respond with the information required by 2031.230. Beasom is ordered to make further responses by May 15, 2025 to all RFPs in set one and to comply with 2031.230 in her amended responses. Anderson seeks sanctions. The court does not award evidentiary sanctions in light of Beasom's supplemental response but does find Beasom's position lacks substantial justification. Beasom shall pay $500 in sanctions to Anderson by May 15, 2025. [END OF TENTTIVE RULING PART 1, SEE PART 2] = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: AIDAN ELIZABETH DIETZ VS. FIVE KEYS SCHOOL AND PROGRAM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Statement Of Damages, And For An Award Of Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 12, DEFENDANT FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAMS' [SUED HEREIN AS FIVE KEYS SCHOOLS AND PROGRAM] Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Verified Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, And Request For Statement Of Damages, And For An Award Of Sanctions Defendant Five Keys Schools and Programs' motion to compel Plaintiff Aidan Elizabeth Dietz to respond to its Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Sets One and Two; and Request for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. Dietz failed to timely respond to the above discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a); 2031.300(a).) Dietz is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by documents responsive to the Requests for Production, within 20 days of entry of this order. Five Keys also seeks sanctions. The relevant discovery statutes require this court to impose sanctions unless the party against whom relief is ordered acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make imposition of sanctions unjust. (CCP 2030.290(c); 2031.300(c).) The court does not find substantial justification but reduces the sanctions amount in view of all the circumstances, including that Dietz's counsel withdrew before discovery responses were provided. The court awards $500 in sanctions, payable by Dietz to Five Keys within 20 days of entry of this order. Five Keys is ordered to re-serve its written discovery requests on Dietz with service of this order. Separately, Five Keys seeks an order compelling Dietz to provide a statement of damages. The court grants the request and orders Dietz to provide "a statement setting forth the nature and amount of damages being sought" to Five Keys within 20 days. (See CCP 425.11.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL CHAN VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Protective Order To Govern Production Of Confidential Materials
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 13, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Motion For Protective Order To Govern Production Of Confidential Materials The court grants in part and denies in part Ford Motor Company's motion for protective order. The parties are in agreement, and Ford's evidence establishes, that a protective order is appropriate in this case. The court adopts the version of the Los Angeles Superior Court model protective order attached as Exhibits F and G to the Maggio Declaration with the following modifications: In paragraph 7.b., the court adopts the "same firm" limitation in the first sentence. This allows any counsel of record in this proceeding (including attorneys from other firms who take on representation of plaintiff Samuel Chan or defendant Ford and enter general appearances) to access the confidential materials. The court rejects the requirement that lawyers and staff within a firm representing Chan or Ford must execute a confidentiality agreement in the last sentence of paragraph 7.b. In paragraph 7.d., the court adopts the changes Ford proposes. This is a common protective measure in commercial litigation for confidential material. In paragraph 7.f., the court rejects the changes Ford proposes. In paragraph 7.g., the court adopts the changes Ford proposes. The court adopts Ford's new proposed 8.a. and 8.b. paragraphs. The court adopts the changes Ford proposes to paragraph 21. This is another common protective measure in commercial litigation for confidential material. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: DESIRAE FERIA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednseday, April 30, 2025, line 7, DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MICHELLE ZAPATA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Michelle Zapata move to strike punitive damages allegations from plaintiff Desirae Feria's First Amended Complaint (FAC). For the reasons stated herein the court denies the motion. The court previously granted the motion to strike. Feria added allegations, notably the allegations that her supervisor Zapata forced her to work as a substitute in violation of her work restrictions, which exacerbated her injury and forced her to begin using crutches. (FAC para. 14.e.) The FAC further alleges that Zapata was aware of Feria's work restrictions but disregarded them in order to cause Feria discomfort and drive her to quit. (Id.) "In order to state a prima facie claim for punitive damages, a complaint must set forth the elements as stated in the general punitive damage statute, Civil Code section 3294. These statutory elements include allegations that the defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud or malice. 'Malice' is defined in the statute as conduct 'intended by the defendant to cause injury to plaintiff, or despicable conduct that is carried on by the defendant with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights or safety of others.' 'Oppression' means despicable conduct that subjects a person to cruel and unjust hardship in conscious disregard of that person's rights.' " (Turman v. Turning Point of Central California, Inc. (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 53, 63 [internal citations and quotation marks omitted].) The court concludes that Feria has now adequately pled the basis for punitive damages in Zapata's alleged knowledge of her injury and intent to exacerbate her injury. The motion to strike is accordingly denied. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
